title: Collection Persistence, Versioned Schemas, Atomic Replacement, and Restore · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers collection persistence, versioned schemas, atomic replacement, and restore into a changed versioned atomic offline collection store. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned atomic offline collection store with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Keep browser lessons on pure byte models and place native filesystem effects behind an injected save and load port.
Define version, application identity, timestamp policy, records, provenance, and checksum or integrity scope with deterministic encoding.
Bound bytes, decode exactly one document, validate schema and every record, reject duplicates, and preserve the last valid in-memory state on error.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Persistence Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 6684 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate versioned collection bytes and prove failure-safe atomic replacement and restore; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then change one resource identity, pagination link, JSON field, status, or body budget and revalidate admission. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep browser lessons on pure byte models and place native filesystem effects behind an injected save and load port.

What must be established before implementation?

Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-migration-restore-tests-1rzhcp" that uses case 8235 to validate versioned collection bytes and prove failure-safe atomic replacement and restore. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
